_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: This indicates you should flip to the back side (B面) of the form to continue filling out additional required information.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.10: This section is for official use only by government clerks - do not write anything in areas marked for clerk/official use (カ記入).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: This code relates to residence record maintenance and official authority actions - this would be filled by immigration officials, not by applicants.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.12: This checkbox section is for documenting reasons for loss or acquisition of eligibility status and related qualification documents. Most foreign residents can leave this blank unless specifically instructed by city hall staff, as it typically relates to changes in insurance or pension eligibility that would be handled directly by the municipal office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.5: Fill in the birth date using Japanese era year (平成/令和), then month and day. For gender, circle 男 (male) or 女 (female). For occupation, circle the appropriate number: 1.会社員 (company employee), 2.自営業 (self-employed), 3.アルバイト (part-time), 4.なし (none), or 5.その他 (other). For the 有・無 (yes/no) fields, circle the appropriate option based on what the form is asking about.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: Write the name of the specific ward office or district office where you are submitting this form. For example, if you live in Shibuya ward, you would write '渋谷' in the parentheses. Check your residence card or ask at the counter if you're unsure which ward office serves your area.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: Write the name of your local ward office or the specific National Health Insurance section where you are submitting this form. You can find this information on your residence card or by checking which ward/district (ku) you live in within your city.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: This is a notification/notice section that provides instructions or important information about the form. You should read this section carefully as it contains guidance about the form completion process, but you typically don't need to write anything in this field - it's informational only.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.5: This field is for notification or contact information, typically where you would write details about how you can be reached or any important notices regarding your application. As a foreign resident, include your current phone number, email address, or any special circumstances the office should know about when processing your form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is a pre-printed form identifier meaning 'Form No. 1' - you don't need to write anything here as it's already filled in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is pre-printed text showing the form is addressed to the 'Mayor of Arakawa Ward' - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write your phone number here in the parentheses, using Japanese format (typically written as 03-1234-5678 for Tokyo numbers or similar local format).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Write the phone number where you can be reached. Include the area code and write in standard Japanese format (e.g., 03-1234-5678 for landlines or 090-1234-5678 for mobile phones).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: Write the end date of your previous visa or status of residence in Japanese date format (year/month/day). If this is your first time in Japan or you're changing from a different status, write the date your current status expires.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: Write the start date when you began your current activities or employment in Japan in Japanese date format (year/month/day). This should be the actual date you started working or engaging in the activities related to your visa status.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.10: Write the phone number of the person responsible for issuing or handling this document/application. Include area code in parentheses if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.11: Write the phone number where you can be reached regarding this application. This should be your own contact number where officials can call you if needed.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.3: Enter the date (year, month, day) when you lost your job or left your previous employment. Use Japanese calendar format (Reiwa/Heisei era) or Western calendar.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.5: Write the company name and address of your previous employer in the first set of parentheses, and your job title or department in the second set of parentheses.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Enter your phone number in the format shown, with area code in parentheses. Use the standard Japanese phone number format with hyphens or parentheses to separate the area code.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: Write your given name (first name) in katakana characters. If you're a foreign resident, use the katakana spelling that matches your residence card or other official Japanese documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: This field is pre-printed and shows who the application is being submitted to (the Mayor of Bunkyo Ward). You don't need to write anything here as it's already filled in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Circle the appropriate numbers to indicate if this is a continuation application (継), renewal application (更), or enter your ID number in the provided boxes. Most first-time applicants can leave this section blank unless specifically instructed otherwise.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: This field is for recording re-entry or moving back in from another municipality or from abroad. Leave this blank unless you are moving back to a place where you were previously registered - if applicable, enter the date you are moving back in using the Japanese date format (year/month/day).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: This field is for recording when someone moves into this municipality from another city/prefecture or from abroad. If you are moving from overseas or another Japanese municipality, the municipal office will typically fill this section with your previous address and move-in date during registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: These bullet points are used to separate different categories or sections of information on the form. Leave these as-is since they are pre-printed formatting elements, not fields to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: These are separator dots that help organize the form layout visually. These are decorative elements that should not be modified or filled in by the applicant.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.2: These are bullet points for listing additional family members. Write the names of all other household members who will be moving with you, one name per bullet point.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: These dots are separators or continuation markers for the list above. Use them to continue listing family members if you need more space than the first set of bullet points provides.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.6: This parenthetical space is for additional notes or clarifications. You can write details like relationship to head of household or special circumstances if needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: Check the appropriate boxes to indicate your moving status - whether you're moving from another city in Japan (転入) or moving back from overseas (再転入). Foreign residents typically check the transfer-in option.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.3: These are bullet points for listing additional family members' names and information that didn't fit in the main sections above. Leave blank if all family members were already recorded in the previous sections.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.4: These are continuation bullet points for listing more family member details if needed. Only fill in if you have additional household members beyond what the main form sections can accommodate.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: These are bullet point separators for listing multiple items or addresses. Leave as dots/bullets to separate different entries in the same field.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: These dots serve as spacers or separators between different data elements. Keep them as formatting markers between the information you enter.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.8: These bullet points are used to separate list items or different pieces of information. Maintain them as separators when filling in multiple related entries.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: This field is for recording when someone moves into this municipality from another city/town in Japan or from abroad. If you are moving from overseas or another Japanese municipality, the municipal office will fill in the relevant date and details when you complete your resident registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.4: Check this box if you are moving into this municipality from another city/town in Japan or from overseas. This applies to foreign residents registering their address for the first time or transferring from another Japanese municipality.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.9: These are bullet point markers for listing multiple items or reasons. Fill in the specific details that apply to your situation in the spaces following these dots.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.10: These dots serve as separators or continuation markers for additional information. Use these spaces to provide supplementary details or multiple entries related to your registration.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.13: These bullet points are for listing specific circumstances or categories that apply to your case. Mark or fill in the relevant options that describe your particular moving situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.5: This field is for residents who are moving back into the municipality from another city/prefecture or from overseas. Only fill this out if you previously lived in this area and are now returning - leave blank if this is your first time registering at this address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.4: This field is for recording when someone moves into this municipality from another city/prefecture or from overseas. Leave blank if this doesn't apply to your situation.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.10: These are bullet point markers for listing instructions or items - no action needed from you as they're part of the form's printed text.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.11: These are decorative separator dots that are part of the form's layout design - you don't need to fill anything in these areas.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.14: These are bullet point markers that precede instructions or list items - they're part of the printed form text, so no input is required from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 9:.3: These are bullet points indicating list items or instructions. You don't need to write anything here - they're just formatting markers for the form's instruction text.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 9:.8: This refers to writing the same information as fields 1, 2, and 3 from elsewhere on the form. Copy exactly what you wrote in those corresponding fields.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 9:.9: This also refers to copying the same information from fields 1, 2, and 3. Ensure consistency by writing identical information to what you provided in those earlier fields.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: Write your full name in katakana characters as it appears on your residence card or other official documents. Foreign residents should use the katakana spelling that matches their registered name in Japanese immigration records.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.8: This appears to be a field for entering a phone number or contact information. Write your primary phone number in Japanese format, typically starting with your area code (like 03 for Tokyo) followed by your number, with hyphens between number groups (e.g., 03-1234-5678).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.2: Write the relationship of this person to the household head, such as '妻' (wife), '子' (child), '父' (father), etc. If this is the household head themselves, write '本人' (self).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.2: Write today's date when you're submitting this form in the format: year, month, day using Japanese calendar (Reiwa era) or Western calendar.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Enter today's date in the boxes provided - typically in Japanese format with year (年), month (月), and day (日).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.4: This is an instruction note explaining that the person moving out must fill out the form themselves, and representatives or other household members cannot submit it.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.5: Write your full name exactly as it appears on your residence card or other official documents in Japan.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.6: This note emphasizes that you (the person moving out) must personally fill out and submit this form - no one else can do it for you.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.7: Enter your phone number using the Japanese format (area code-exchange-number), typically written with circles, triangles, and squares as placeholders (○○○-△△△△-□□□□).
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: This instruction asks you to provide a phone number where you can be easily reached during business hours for any follow-up questions.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.9: Write your current full address in Itabashi ward, including the district name, street number, and apartment/mansion name and room number if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: Write the name of the head of household (usually the person whose name is on the main residence registration) - this may be you or another family member.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.11: If you live in an apartment or mansion, make sure to include the building name and room number as shown in the example.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.10: This is an instruction bubble stating that you must fill in information for all household members who are moving out, including any family members or dependents. Make sure to complete the required fields for every person leaving the residence, not just yourself.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.12: This is a declaration statement meaning 'I hereby submit this notification as stated above.' You don't need to write anything here - it's simply confirming that you are officially submitting the form with all the information you've provided.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 5:.5: This is a revision date stamp indicating the form was last updated on October 13, 2024 (Reiwa 6). You don't need to write anything here - it's just for reference to show which version of the form you're using.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write the title of the form '住民異動届出書' (Resident Registration Change Notification Form) at the top of the document.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write '東京都北区長 あて' (To: Mayor of Kita Ward, Tokyo) to address the form to the appropriate authority.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write '※太線の枠内のみお書きください' (Please write only within the thick-lined boxes) as an instruction note.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Enter the date you are submitting this form in Japanese format (年/月/日 - year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Check the box for '転居(北区内で引越した方)' if you moved within Kita Ward, as this is one of the main reasons foreigners use this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Check '転入(北区外から引越してきた方)' if you moved to Kita Ward from outside the ward - the most common situation for new foreign residents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Check '世帯主変更' if there's a change in the head of household, or check the 'その他' (other) box if your situation doesn't fit the standard categories.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write your apartment or building name (マンション名等) - include the full name of your apartment building or residence complex.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write '住所' (address) as the field label for where you will enter your registered address information.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write '届出人' (applicant/person filing) as the label - this refers to you as the person submitting this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write '電話' (telephone) as the field label where you will enter your contact phone number.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Enter your phone number in parentheses, including area code - use the format commonly used in Japan.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write 'フリガナ' (furigana) as the label for the phonetic reading of names in katakana script.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write '(窓口に来られた方)' meaning '(person who came to the counter)' - this refers to whoever is physically submitting the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write '※届出人の署名が必要です。' (The applicant's signature is required) as an important notice about the signature requirement.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Check this box if you are moving out of this municipality to another location. You would use this when leaving your current registered address to move elsewhere in Japan or abroad.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: Check this box if you are moving into this municipality from another city/town in Japan or from abroad. This is the most common selection for foreign residents establishing residence.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.10: Enter your new postal code (zip code) for your address in Japan. Japanese postal codes are 7 digits in XXX-XXXX format.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.1: This appears to be a postal code field. Write the 7-digit postal code for the address, replacing the circles with the actual numbers (e.g., 132-0034).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.10: This appears to be a date field showing year, month, and day in Japanese format (Reiwa era). Write the relevant date replacing the numbers shown (63 likely refers to Reiwa 6, which is 2024).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.3: This appears to be a date field showing '63年6月6日' (June 6, Showa 63/1988). Enter the relevant date using the Japanese calendar system - Showa 63 corresponds to 1988 in the Western calendar. If you're unsure about Japanese era conversions, you can ask at the municipal office or use online conversion tools.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is a formal address line meaning 'To the Mayor of Minato Ward' - you don't need to fill this in as it's already printed on the form. This indicates the form is being submitted to the Minato Ward office.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Enter the date when you moved out of your previous address. Write the year, month, and day in the format shown (年月日). If you're moving from overseas, this would be when you left your foreign residence.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.7: This appears to be a checkbox or selection field asking about English language ability or preference. Check or select '有' (yes) if you want English language support or if you have English proficiency, or leave unmarked if not applicable.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Check whether you have a health insurance card (健康保険証) - most foreign residents should check '有' (Yes) if enrolled in National Health Insurance or employee insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: Write the pension number or identifier if you have enrolled in the Japanese pension system - leave blank if you haven't joined yet.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: Check whether you have other relevant documents like pension handbook or residence card - check '有' (Yes) if you possess any additional official documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: This appears to be a field for additional information or notes related to the form entry. Leave blank unless you have specific circumstances to note that don't fit in other sections.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: This appears to be asking about English language ability or English-related qualifications. Select '有' (yes) if you have English proficiency or relevant English certifications.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.13: This is another English-related field, likely asking about English language skills or certifications. Mark '有' (yes) if applicable to your language abilities or educational background.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.14: Write the name of your home country in Japanese katakana or kanji characters (e.g., アメリカ for America, イギリス for UK). If unsure of the Japanese writing, ask at the municipal office counter.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.15: Circle '有' (yes) if you have English proficiency or '無' (no) if you don't. This appears to be asking about English language ability, which may be relevant for certain services or documentation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.1: This appears to be asking about English language ability or preference. Mark the appropriate box to indicate whether you can use English or prefer English language services when available.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: Circle or mark whether you have a My Number card and if so, whether it's currently active (運用中) or temporarily suspended (一時停止). If you don't have a My Number card, leave this blank or mark accordingly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: This appears to be a continuation or second instance of the My Number card status question - mark the same status as field 4, indicating whether your card is in use or temporarily suspended.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.10: Select whether you have 2 available cards and indicate if they are currently in operation or temporarily suspended. Choose the appropriate status based on your current card usage situation.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.11: Select whether you have 5 available items/cards and indicate their operational status - either actively in use or temporarily suspended. Mark the option that matches your actual current status.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.4: Select whether the My Number card is currently in operation (運用中) or temporarily suspended (一時停止). Most foreign residents with active cards should select 'in operation' unless they have specifically requested suspension of their card's functions.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.6: Select the current operational status of the property - choose '運用中' (in operation) if the property is actively being used/rented, or '一時停止' (temporarily suspended) if it's currently not in use but still available. Most residential properties should be marked as '運用中' unless there's a specific reason they're temporarily out of service.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write '転出届' (Moving Out Notification) as the title of this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write the name of your current ward/city mayor or office head, typically found on the ward office website or ask staff for the correct title.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Write today's date in Japanese format: year, month, day (西暦 or 令和年 both acceptable).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write that you are moving out from Ota Ward - state your reason such as '転居のため' (due to relocation).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This instruction reminds you to use your official registered name (as it appears on your residence card).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write your full legal name exactly as it appears on your residence card or other official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write your current phone number where you can be reached during business hours.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write your mobile phone number as an alternative contact method.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write your email address for any follow-up communications from the ward office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: For international moves, write only the country name in Japanese (e.g., アメリカ for USA).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your new complete address where you are moving to, including prefecture, city, and detailed address.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write the name of the head of household at your new address (could be yourself if living alone).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write your current registered address in Ota Ward exactly as it appears on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write the district number and building number of your current address (丁目、番、号).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write the name of the current head of household at your current address (yourself if you're the head).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write the date when you moved out of your previous address in Japan using the format year/month/day. If you're moving from overseas to Japan for the first time, you can leave this blank or write the date you left your home country.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.1: Write the actual date you moved out of your previous address in Japan, using the format Year/Month/Day. This should be the date you physically left your old residence.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: If you haven't moved yet, write your planned moving date here instead of field 1. Use this field when submitting the form before your actual move date.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Include a return envelope with proper postage, and write your postal code, address, and name on it so the certificate can be mailed back to you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Provide copies of identity documents such as driver's license (front and back), My Number card (front only), passport, health insurance qualification certificate (with ID numbers hidden), or pension book - at least one document is required.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: This is a continuation of the identity document requirements from field 2.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: This is a continuation of the identity document requirements from field 2.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.5: This is a continuation of the identity document requirements from field 2.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This shows where to submit your application - you don't need to fill this in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This is the official address: 〒144-8621 Tokyo, Ota-ku, Kamata 5-chome 13-14, Ota Ward Office, Family Registration Division, Mail Section.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: This is a continuation of the submission address from field 7.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: This is the phone number section header - you don't need to fill this in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: This is the contact phone number 03-5744-1676 for inquiries - you don't need to fill this in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: This explains that certificates will arrive about one week after submitting your application, so apply with sufficient time allowance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.12: This is a continuation of the timing information from field 11.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: This explains that for scheduled moves, you can apply 2 weeks before your moving date, and asks for your consideration regarding postal delivery timing.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: This is a continuation of the moving information from field 13.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.15: This note explains that transfer certificates will be sent to either your previous address or new address depending on timing.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is a notice section informing you of important restrictions and requirements. You don't need to write anything here - it's just providing information that mail forwarding services cannot handle these procedures, moving/address change notifications are only accepted at the counter, and documents must be filled out by yourself (word processing is not allowed).

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Write the name of the head of household in the designated field. This should be written in Japanese characters (hiragana, katakana, or kanji) and must match the name registered in your residence records.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.4: Check this box if you are moving to a new address within the same city/municipality (not moving from a different city). This is different from 転入 (moving in from another municipality).
_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.8: This section is for people who are moving in from another municipality or moving within the same municipality. Fill out the required procedures and documentation for postal forwarding services if needed.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.1: This is the form title indicating it's a notification for moving in/out of a residence - no action needed as it's pre-printed.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.2: This shows the municipality name (Setagaya City) where you're registering - this is pre-printed and indicates which city office you're dealing with.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.4: Enter your date of birth in the Japanese format (year/month/day). Use the Japanese calendar year (Reiwa, Heisei, etc.) or Western calendar as specified on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 1:.12: Enter the date of birth for another household member (likely spouse or family member) using the same Japanese date format as field 4.

_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.3: Write your date of birth in the format specified on the form (likely DD/MM/YYYY or MM/DD/YYYY). Make sure it matches exactly what's on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.5: Write your full name in Roman alphabet letters exactly as it appears on your residence card, including any middle names or initials. Do not use katakana or any other script.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.10: Enter your full name in Roman alphabet letters exactly as shown on your residence card. This should match field 5 and any other name fields on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 5 - Section 2:.11: Write your complete name in Roman alphabet letters as it appears on your residence card. Ensure consistency with all other name fields throughout the document.

_vision_explanations:Page 6 - Section 1:.1: This field is pre-printed and addressed to the Mayor of Setagaya Ward - you don't need to fill anything here as it's already completed.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.11: This indicates that online registration is available for this service. If you prefer to register online instead of submitting a paper form, you can use the QR codes or online system mentioned, which may be more convenient for foreign residents who can use translation tools.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: This is a pre-printed phone number (050-3816-3100) for the Tokyo Main Office of the Light Motor Vehicle Inspection Organization. You don't need to write anything here - it's provided for your reference to contact them if you own a light motor vehicle.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: This is a pre-printed phone number (050-5540-2030) for the Kanto Transport Bureau's Tokyo Transport Branch. You don't need to write anything here - it's provided for your reference to contact them if you own a motorcycle of 125cc or larger.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: This field shows the office hours when government services are available. No action needed - this is informational only.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: This QR code links to garbage separation and collection information. Scan it to learn local recycling rules and pickup schedules.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: This QR code provides information about facilities within the ward. Scan to find nearby community centers, libraries, and other public facilities.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.12: This shows the standard office hours (8:30 AM to 5:00 PM) for government services. No action needed - this is reference information.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.14: This indicates community center information is available. Look for related QR codes or contact details to find your nearest center.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.15: This refers to garbage separation rules information. Use the associated QR code to access detailed sorting guidelines for your area.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: This is the representative phone number (代表番号) for the Shinagawa ward office, which is 03-3777-1111. This is a reference number already filled in - you don't need to write anything here as it's the contact information for the government office itself.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: This is the phone number for Shinagawa Ward office (03-3777-1111) that you can call for assistance with the moving-in process during business hours 8:30-17:00.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: This indicates you need to submit a 'Moving-in Notification' (転入届) form, which is required when you move to Shinagawa Ward from another municipality or from abroad.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: This is just a copyright notice for the Cinnamoroll character mascot used in the ward's promotional materials - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.8: This completes the sentence about the moving-in notification being required for submission - it's part of the instructional text explaining the mandatory paperwork.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: This means 'or' and indicates you can choose alternative documentation options. It connects the driver's license option above with other acceptable ID documents like My Number card or residence card combinations listed after this marker.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.6: This lists the Shinagawa First Regional Center location and phone number - you don't need to fill anything here, it's just reference information for where you can complete procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.7: This shows the Oi First Regional Center address - again, this is just informational text showing another location where procedures can be completed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.8: This is the phone number for the Oi First Regional Center - no action needed, just reference information.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.9: This lists the Ebara Fourth Regional Center address - another optional location for completing your procedures, no writing required.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.10: This is the phone number for the Ebara Fourth Regional Center - keep this for reference if you need to call them.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: This introduces the next section about required procedures - read the following instructions carefully to understand what documents or steps come next.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: This is an informational notice stating that other procedures or forms are located on the inside pages of the document. You don't need to write anything here - it's just directing you to look at the inner pages for additional forms or steps you may need to complete.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.5: This is an informational note stating that automatic renewal will occur, so no procedures are required. You don't need to write anything here - it's just explaining that if you're receiving pension payments, the process will automatically update without any action needed from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.3: This field states that automatic renewal will occur so no procedures are required. You don't need to write anything here - it's just informational text indicating that your status will be automatically updated without any action needed on your part.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This field is pre-printed and indicates the form is addressed to the Mayor of Shinjuku City - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Check this box if you are the head of household (main person registered at the address) filling out the form for yourself.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: This indicates the type of form - it's a pre-printed title meaning 'Notification of Change in Residence' so no action needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Check this box if you are moving from one address to another within Shinjuku City (same municipality).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Check this box if you are moving into Shinjuku City from a different municipality in Japan or from abroad.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write your full name clearly by hand in Japanese characters (katakana for foreign names) - this should match your residence card exactly.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This appears to be a grid for selecting the type of registration you're applying for (transfer, change, divorce, merger, certificate update, etc.). Mark the appropriate boxes that correspond to your specific application type.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This section is for the notification date and effective date of your registration change. Fill in the year, month, and day in the provided boxes using the Japanese calendar format or Western calendar as specified on the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Only fill this out if someone else is submitting this form on your behalf. Write the proxy person's full address and their relationship to you (such as spouse, family member, legal representative, etc.) in clear Japanese or romaji letters.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.2: Only fill this out if someone else is submitting this form on your behalf. Write the proxy's full address and their relationship to you (e.g., spouse, parent, legal representative).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: Write your complete new address including postal code, prefecture, city, ward/district, and apartment/building information. Use the official Japanese address format as it appears on your residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Check the appropriate box to indicate if you are the head of household at your new address or if someone else is the head of household. This affects tax and administrative records.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: This field is marked 'For Japanese nationals only' so foreign residents should leave this completely blank.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This appears to be part of an education/qualification section where you would indicate if you attended medical elementary school - most people would leave this blank unless specifically applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.12: This marking likely indicates a gender field where 'M' stands for male - mark or check this if you are male, otherwise leave blank if female options are elsewhere on the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: This appears to be a checkbox or selection field marked with '・(M)' which likely indicates a required field related to gender/sex designation. Select or mark the appropriate option as indicated by the form instructions, typically choosing between male (男) or female (女) options that should be visible in this section.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.7: This appears to be a checkbox or selection field marked with (M) indicating it's likely mandatory. Check the box or select the appropriate option based on the context of the surrounding form fields - you may need to refer to instructions elsewhere on the form to understand what this specific selection represents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.9: Write the contact method in parentheses, such as '電話' (telephone), 'メール' (email), or '郵送' (mail) to indicate how you prefer to be contacted regarding this form or application.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.9: Check this box or mark it if you are returning or have returned the card/document being referenced in this form. This is typically used when you're submitting a replacement application and need to return your old card or when you're leaving Japan and returning your residence card.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.3: Leave this field blank unless you specifically attended a medical elementary school, which is extremely rare in Japan. This field is only for those who completed elementary education at a specialized medical institution.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: Mark this circle if the person is male. This appears to be part of a gender selection where you choose between male (M) or female options.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.9: Leave this field blank unless you attended a medical elementary school, which is extremely rare in Japan. This applies only to specialized medical preparatory education institutions.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.15: This appears to be a checkbox or marker field related to male gender classification. Mark or check this field if it applies to your gender designation on official documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 3:.3: This appears to be a checkbox or marker for male (M) gender designation. If you are male, mark or check this field; if female, you would mark the corresponding female field instead.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.12: Select 'Yes' if any applicant was previously registered as a resident in Japan, or 'No' if this is their first time registering. This refers to prior registration in the residential registry system, not tourist visits.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 4:.14: Check this box and write '済' (completed) only after you have finished filling out the entire form and reviewed all sections for accuracy.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is an informational notice that online moving notifications started on February 6, 2023 - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This refers to the 'moving out notification' form you need to submit when leaving your current address.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: This indicates where you submit your moving out notification - at the municipal office counter in person.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: This refers to having to visit both the old and new municipal office counters when moving between different cities.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: This acknowledges the inconvenience of having to visit multiple offices in person for address changes.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This indicates you need to submit a moving-out notification (転出届) at your current city/ward office before moving to a new municipality. You must do this in person and will receive a transfer certificate (転出証明書) that you'll need when registering at your new address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: This section explains that you need to submit a moving-in notification (転入届) at the municipal office of your new address. You should go to the municipal office counter in person to complete this process - simply going to the office window is sufficient to handle this requirement.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.8: This refers to office opening hours at municipal offices. You don't need to fill this in - it's explanatory text indicating that you can submit moving notifications online without worrying about when government offices are open.

_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.5: This appears to be describing a scenario rather than a field to fill out - it's explaining that people with no days off can still use the 24/7 online service to complete their moving procedures.
_vision_explanations:Page 3 - Section 1:.9: This is also descriptive text explaining that you can submit your moving-out notification (転出届) during spare time like after work on weekdays, thanks to the 24/7 online system availability.

_vision_explanations:Page 4 - Section 1:.1: This section contains frequently asked questions about the My Number portal and moving notifications. Review these Q&As to understand who can submit notifications, whether family members can submit together, proxy submission rules, and future requirements for using the portal.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This indicates the form was downloaded from an online service - no action needed, just informational text.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This is pre-printed text addressing the form to the Mayor of Taito Ward - leave as is, no writing required.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Write the full name of the 3rd person in your household exactly as it appears on their residence card or official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Enter the birth date of the 3rd person using the Japanese calendar format (年/月/日 for year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: Write the full name of the 4th person in your household exactly as it appears on their residence card or official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: Enter the birth date of the 4th person using the Japanese calendar format (年/月/日 for year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.5: Write the full name of the 5th person in your household exactly as it appears on their residence card or official documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: Enter the birth date of the 5th person using the Japanese calendar format (年/月/日 for year/month/day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This note explains that if you have 6 or more people, you need to print another form and fill in starting from person #5.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: This section header indicates the documents you need to submit along with this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: Follow the instructions below for items ① and ② and mail them to the designated submission address.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: Check this box to indicate you are submitting the moving-out notification form (this main document).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.11: Check this box if you are including copies of identity verification documents for the person moving out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.12: This explains the point system for identity documents - you need either A(1 point), B(2 points), or B(1 point)+C(2 points).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: Category A includes My Number card, driver's license, passport, residence card, etc. - high-value ID documents.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: Check this box if you are moving overseas and have already left Japan. This indicates you need to submit additional documentation.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.2: Include a copy of your passport showing your photo and the departure stamp from Japan when you left the country.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.3: If there's no departure stamp in your passport, attach copies of airline tickets or other documents that prove your departure date from Japan.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.4: This note explains that if you don't have a departure stamp, you should include flight tickets or similar proof of your departure date.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: This shows the submission address - you don't need to fill this in as it's pre-printed information.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.6: This is the pre-printed recipient address (Taito Ward Office) - no action needed from you as it's already filled in.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write the area code in parentheses for the telephone number provided above. For example, if your phone number is 03-1234-5678, write (03) in the parentheses.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Enter the date of application submission in the format Year-Month-Day. Use the Japanese calendar era (令和/Reiwa) or Western calendar (平成/Heisei) as indicated by the checkbox you selected above.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Write the full name of the person who rents/leases the property (the tenant). This should match the name on your rental contract exactly as it appears in katakana, hiragana, or kanji.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.12: Enter your phone number, typically your mobile phone number. Write it in the standard Japanese format without hyphens, such as 09012345678, or with hyphens like 090-1234-5678 depending on the form's preference.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.9: Write 'F' followed by your visa status code in parentheses, such as 'F(技術・人文知識・国際業務)' for skilled worker visa or 'F(永住者)' for permanent resident.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.15: This appears to be a duplicate field - enter the same visa status information as field 9, writing 'F' followed by your current residence status in parentheses.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.12: This is a pre-printed checkbox that indicates whether you've received a notice about your reporting period expiring. If you have received such a notice from the municipal office, check '済' (completed). Most foreign residents won't need to check this unless they've specifically received an expiration notice for their residence registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.1: This is just a header indicating this is a 'Change of Residence' form - no need to fill anything in this field.
_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 1:.13: Write 'F' if you are female, or leave blank if you already indicated male (M) in the adjacent gender field.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.6: Write the furigana (phonetic reading) for any kanji characters in your name using hiragana or katakana. If your name is already written in alphabet/katakana, you can leave this blank or write the phonetic reading in katakana.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Select your preferred payment method for health insurance premiums, typically either automatic bank transfer (口座振替) which is most convenient, or payment slips sent by mail (納付書). Most foreign residents choose automatic bank transfer to avoid missing payments.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: Check this box if you're submitting this form along with a payment slip (納付書). Leave unchecked if no payment is being made with this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.9: Enter dates in YYYY-MM-DD format using the dashes as separators. Use Japanese calendar years (Reiwa era) or Western calendar as specified in the form instructions.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.10: Enter dates in YYYY-MM-DD format using the dashes as separators. Ensure consistency with the date format used elsewhere in the form (Japanese era or Western calendar).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.8: Write the date when this form was filled out or submitted, using the Japanese date format with dots as separators (year・month・day). For example, if filling out on March 15, 2024, write '2024・3・15' or use the Japanese era year format like 'R6・3・15' (Reiwa 6th year).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: Enter your date of birth in Japanese format (Showa/Heisei/Reiwa era year, month, day). For example, if born in 1990, write 'Heisei 2' for the era year.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: Enter your date of birth again in the same Japanese era format. This appears to be for a second person or family member on the same form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.5: Enter your birth date in the Japanese format using dots as separators (year・month・day). For example, if born on March 15, 1990, write '1990・3・15' or use the Japanese era system like 'Heisei 2・3・15'.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.11: Enter your birth date in the same Japanese format with dots as separators (year・month・day). This appears to be for a second person on the form, so use the same date format as field 5 but for the other individual being registered.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.6: These are separator dots between date fields (year, month, day). Leave these dots as they are - they're pre-printed separators, so you only need to fill in the numbers for the date in the adjacent boxes.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.1: Enter the date when you are preparing/filling out this form. Use the Japanese date format (year/month/day) or the format specified in the form instructions.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.2: Enter the date when you created or prepared this form. Write it in the Japanese format: year/month/day (YYYY/MM/DD) or year年month月day日.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.11: These are separator dots or bullet points - leave them as is, they're just formatting elements on the form and don't require any input from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.15: These are separator dots or bullet points - leave them as is, they're just formatting elements on the form and don't require any input from you.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.4: Write '動' (dō) to indicate this is a change/movement notification form, such as moving residences or changing personal information with the government.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: These are separator dots used for formatting - leave blank or add dots/periods as visual separators between different sections of information if required by the form layout.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.2: Write the specific type of change or movement you are reporting, such as address change, status change, or family composition change. Be specific and use the exact terminology from official guidelines.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.3: Fill in the date fields using the format day/month/year or as specified in the form instructions. Make sure dates are consistent with supporting documents you're submitting.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.6: Enter relevant dates related to your reported change, ensuring they align chronologically with your situation. Double-check that all date entries follow the required Japanese date format.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.10: Complete any additional date fields required for your specific type of change or movement. These dates should correspond to when events occurred or when changes became effective.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.14: Fill in final date-related information, which may include deadlines, effective dates, or completion dates. Ensure all dates throughout the form are accurate and verifiable with your supporting documentation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is the title section explaining the special procedure for moving out using My Number Card by mail. No input required here.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This explains that no moving-out certificate will be issued when using My Number Card for the move, and you must bring your My Number Card and enter the 4-digit verification number at your new address for the moving-in procedure.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This states that if you don't submit your moving-in notification within 14 days of moving or 30 days of your scheduled move date (whichever is earlier), you'll need to attach a moving-out certificate to your moving-in notification.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This instructs you to fill out the required information in the moving-out notification form below and send it with necessary documents to the city hall.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This indicates the first required document: the completed moving-out notification form (shown below).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: This indicates the second required document: copies of identity verification materials such as My Number Card, driver's license, or health insurance qualification certificate for the person submitting the notification.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: This is the first important note: you cannot complete this procedure if you don't have a valid My Number Card on hand.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: This is the second important note: if someone other than the person moving is handling the procedure at the new address, they must bring a power of attorney (委任状).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: This shows the contact information for inquiries: Ashiya City Hall, Citizens' Life Department, telephone 659-8501, address in Hyogo Prefecture.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write the date you are submitting this notification form, not the date you plan to move out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your full name exactly as it appears on your My Number Card or residence card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write your current address exactly as registered in the resident registry, including apartment numbers if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write your current address exactly as registered in the resident registry, including apartment numbers if applicable.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: Write the date you plan to move out of your current address in year/month/day format using Japanese calendar or Western calendar as specified by the form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: This section is for specifying the time period or range of your move, which may be used if you have a flexible moving schedule.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.8: This field shows 'Revised 202512' indicating this form version was revised in December 2025. This is pre-printed information that you don't need to fill out - it's just the form version number for administrative purposes.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: This is the form title indicating it's a 'Resident Registration Change Notification (Example)' - you don't need to fill this in as it's just the header.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Enter the first part of your phone number (area code). In this example, '079' represents the Kobe area code - replace with your actual area code.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Enter the remaining digits of your phone number after the area code. Make sure to include a number where the municipal office can reach you during business hours.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Enter the planned date when you will move out of your current address in the format year/month/day using Japanese calendar era or Western calendar. If you're not planning to move out, leave this field blank as it only applies to people who are relocating.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: Write the date you plan to move out of your current address in Japanese date format (era year, month, day). If you're not planning to move out, leave this field blank - this section is only for people who are leaving this address.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.6: Check the appropriate box for notification type - likely 'individual' (個) for personal matters. This indicates whether this is an individual or group notification.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.9: Check the relevant boxes for documents you need - transfer certificate (転出証明), mother-child handbook (母子手帳), birth certificate (誕生記念), etc. Only check boxes for documents that apply to your situation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.3: This section is for recording official documents received or processed, such as moving-out certificates (転出証明), mother-child health handbooks (母子手帳), or birth certificates (誕生記念). Check the appropriate boxes or write in the relevant document types that apply to your situation - foreign residents would most commonly deal with moving-out certificates when relocating between municipalities.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.5: Write the date you plan to move out of your current address (transfer out date) in Japanese date format (year/month/day). This field is only filled if you're moving to a different municipality or overseas, not for moves within the same city.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.6: This appears to be a checkbox or selection field for notification/individual status. You would typically check or mark this if you are filing as an individual person rather than as part of a household or organization, or if you want to receive individual notifications about your application status.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.2: Check the box for '通知' (notification) if you want to be notified of the processing status, or '個' (individual) if this is for personal records. Most foreign residents should check '通知' to receive confirmation of their application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 8:.5: Check the relevant boxes for any certificates or documents you need to transfer or receive, such as '転出証明' (moving-out certificate) if relocating, or '母子手帳' (maternal health handbook) if applicable. Only check boxes for services you actually need.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.7: This is an informational notice, not a field to fill out. It informs you that your seal registration (inkan touroku) will be automatically canceled when you move out of the municipality.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.13: This is also informational text, not a fillable field. It explains that you will be notified by phone or email when the moving-out process is complete, so make sure your contact information is accurate in other parts of the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.1: This is informational text stating that you will be notified by phone or email when your application is processed. No action needed - this is just letting you know how you'll receive updates.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 4:.7: This is a legal warning about providing false information on the form. No action needed - this is just informing you that submitting false information is subject to criminal penalties under Japanese law.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.15: Check this box if you are moving out of your current address to a different municipality. This is used when you need to register a change of address that involves leaving the current city/ward where this form is being submitted.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.5: Write the date you moved out of your previous address in Japan. If this is your first registration in Japan, leave this field blank as it only applies to people who previously lived at a different address within Japan.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is a pre-printed address line showing who the form is being submitted to - the Mayor of Kakogawa City, Hyogo Prefecture. You don't need to write anything here as it's already filled in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: This section indicates the type of move you're making. Check the appropriate box - 転入 means moving into this city from another municipality within Japan, while other options include international moves or transfers within the city.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.3: Check this box only if you previously submitted a moving-out notification (転出届) but need to cancel it because you decided not to move after all. Most foreign residents will leave this unchecked as it's only for canceling a previous moving-out registration.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 5:.11: Select the type of household change you're reporting by marking the appropriate circle - whether you're changing the head of household (世帯主変更), merging households (世帯合併), splitting from a household (世帯分離), or other household structure changes (世帯変更). This section is only relevant if you're making changes to your registered household composition.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 6:.13: Circle the appropriate number (1-5) that corresponds to the type of change you are making to your individual number card information, as referenced in the accompanying instructions or legend provided with the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 7:.1: Check this box if you are moving out of your current municipality to a different city, town, or village. This is the most common option for people relocating to a new area within Japan.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: This text explains that the number of elderly people aged 70 and above increased 1.3 times - informational content only, no form input needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: This shows the 2015 medical expenses total of 42.3 trillion yen - statistical reference data, no action required from form filler.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: This indicates a 1.3x multiplier in the chart - graphical element for reference, not a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: This shows the 1995 medical expenses of 27.0 trillion yen - historical data for comparison, no input needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This explains that by 2025 all baby boomers will be 75+ years old - contextual information about Japan's aging society, no form input required.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: This projects total national medical expenses will reach 61.8 trillion yen by 2025 - statistical projection for reference only.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.4: This refers to the unified operational policy established within each prefecture for handling national health insurance. _vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: This is part of an explanatory sentence stating that prefectures and municipalities will each play their respective roles. This is informational text, not a field to fill out.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: This concludes an explanation about changes to the health insurance system that took effect from fiscal year 2018. This is descriptive text explaining the system reforms, not something you need to complete.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.5: This is a header statement about National Health Insurance being the last safety net - purely informational, no response needed.

_vision_explanations:Page 2 - Section 2:.12: This indicates topics covered by the Management Section including high-cost medical benefits and childbirth lump-sum payments - contact them if you need information about these specific benefits.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write '受付' (reception) - this is a stamp/marking area for office use, leave blank when submitting.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write the current Reiwa year (e.g., '6' for Reiwa 6/2024). Find the current Reiwa year by subtracting 2018 from the current Western year.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This field is pre-filled showing 'Reiwa' (令和), the current Japanese era name - no action needed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is pre-filled addressing the Mayor of Ashiya City - no action needed, it's the recipient of your application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Write your city's regulations reference - likely pre-filled or provided by the office, check with city hall if blank.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Check this box and fill details if you receive continued health insurance benefits under Article 104 or agree to verification of previous insurance.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write 'Reiwa' (令和) for the current era name.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write the current year in Reiwa era (e.g., '6' for 2024).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write the current month and day when submitting the application.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write your full address as registered on your residence card (住所).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your phone number including area code for contact purposes.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: Write your full name exactly as it appears on your residence card, using katakana if you're a foreign resident.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: Write your relationship to the deceased person (e.g., spouse=配偶者, child=子, parent=親).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This note explains that a power of attorney is needed if the person submitting differs from the applicant - relevant if someone else submits for you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.15: Write your full name again as the representative/person submitting the form.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.13: This is just an informational note explaining that if you fill in bank account information below, payments will be prioritized to that account. No action needed - just read and understand.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.15: Enter the bank code (4-digit number) for your bank. You can find this on your bank book, cash card, or by searching your bank's name online along with '銀行コード' (bank code).

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Enter the 4-digit bank code for your chosen bank. This code can be found on your bank's website or by calling them directly.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Enter the 3-digit branch code for the specific branch where your account is held. This is usually printed on your bank book or cash card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.9: Check this box if you want to designate a savings account (貯蓄預金) for the transfer. This is different from a regular checking account and typically has restrictions on withdrawals.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.1: Check this box if you cannot confirm the documents attached above, or if the account name differs from the applicant's name.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.2: Check '葬祭者申立書' if submitting a funeral organizer's written statement, or '葬祭者本人確認書類写し' if submitting a copy of the funeral organizer's ID documents.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: Check the appropriate boxes for delegation documents: '受領委任状兼誓約書' for power of attorney, '委任者本人確認書類写し' for delegator's ID copy, or '受任者本人確認書類写し' for delegate's ID copy.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.4: Write the year, month, and day when you first enrolled in National Health Insurance (not when you moved or changed jobs).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.5: Check '3か月超' if you've been enrolled for more than 3 months, or '3か月以内' if less than 3 months, then specify your insurance type (employee insurance, dependent, other national insurance, or national mutual aid).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.6: Select your benefit status: receiving sickness/maternity allowance, ended benefits within 3 months, or never received benefits.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.10: This is an important note explaining special cases - if the deceased died within 3 months of National Health Insurance enrollment or while receiving certain benefits, special rules may apply to this funeral expense claim.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: Write the title of the form: '国民健康保険特定疾病認定申請書' (Application for National Health Insurance Specified Disease Recognition). This is pre-printed and should not be changed.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write today's date in the Japanese era format 'Reiwa __ year __ month __ day'. For example, 2024 would be Reiwa 6.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This field is pre-printed addressing the Mayor of Ashiya City and should not be changed. It indicates this form is being submitted to Ashiya City's health insurance office.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write your current residential address in Japan exactly as it appears on your residence card or alien registration.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Write your full name as it appears on your residence card. Foreign residents should write their name in katakana if kanji is not available.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write your individual number (My Number) from your My Number card. This is the 12-digit identification number issued to all residents of Japan.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: This is an introductory phrase meaning 'I apply as follows' and is pre-printed. Do not write anything here.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: Write your health insurance number (被保険者番号) exactly as it appears on your National Health Insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.9: Write your full name again, exactly as it appears on your health insurance card.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.10: Write your date of birth in Japanese format: year, month, day. Use the Japanese era system (Showa, Heisei, or Reiwa).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.11: Write your individual number (My Number) again. This should be the same 12-digit number as field 6.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.12: This shows disease option 1: 'Chronic kidney failure requiring artificial kidney (dialysis)'. Check this if applicable to your condition.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.13: This is the label for 'Disease Name' section. The specific diseases are listed as numbered options below.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.14: This shows disease option 2: 'Hemophilia (blood clotting disorder)'. Check this if applicable to your condition.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write the name of the government office or agency where you are submitting this form.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: Circle either 学 (student) or 遠 (distant/other) to indicate your insurance category status.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: This is the header for National Health Insurance - no action needed as it's just a label.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.6: Write the title of the government official this form is addressed to, typically the mayor of your city (市長).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.7: Write today's date in Japanese format (year, month, day).
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.8: In the birth date field, circle the appropriate era: 昭 (Showa), 平 (Heisei), 令 (Reiwa), or 西暦 (Western calendar) and fill in your birth year, month, and day.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 2:.6: Write the number of years you plan to study or have been studying. This refers to the duration of your educational program that requires the student visa.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 3:.3: Write the total number of years you plan to study or have been studying. This should match the duration of your study program or course length as indicated in your enrollment documentation.

_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.1: This is a pre-printed form number (Form No. 13) that identifies this specific type of application form for reissuing National Health Insurance qualification certificates and related documents - you don't need to fill this in.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.2: Write the name of the office or department where you are submitting this application, typically your local city hall's health insurance division.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.3: This is the pre-printed title of the form meaning 'Application for Reissuance of National Health Insurance Qualification Certificate, etc.' - no action needed from you.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.4: Write the title of the official you're addressing, typically '市長' (mayor) preceded by your city name, such as '○○市長' where ○○ is your city's name.
_vision_explanations:Page 1 - Section 1:.5: Enter your date of birth in the Japanese format, first selecting the appropriate era (昭和/平成/令和/西暦) by circling it, then writing the year, month, and day.
